content: Schema week. Courses, modules, enrollments, submissions — 14 tables in Drizzle with RLS policies mirrored in SQL migrations so self-hosters aren't locked to any one Postgres provider.
Gradebook v1 done: weighted categories, drop-lowest, and CSV export that opens cleanly in Excel AND LibreOffice (the BOM matters, who knew).

Rough day. Tried to make SCORM 1.2 and 2004 share one import pipeline and it collapsed under the differences in sequencing rules. Ripping it out and writing two importers with a shared manifest parser instead.

problem_solutions.problem: A unified SCORM importer kept sprouting version conditionals until every function branched on spec version.
problem_solutions.solution: Split into scorm12/ and scorm2004/ importers behind one interface; only the imsmanifest.xml parser is shared. Each importer is now boring and testable.

content: Wrote a manifest fuzzer that mutates real imsmanifest.xml exports — swaps encodings, truncates CDATA, duplicates identifiers. It found two panics and one infinite loop in an afternoon.

problem_solutions.problem: A manifest with a self-referencing organization node sent the tree walker into an infinite loop and pegged the CPU.
problem_solutions.solution: Track visited identifiers in a Set during traversal and bail with a validation error on the first repeat.

content: Demo instance deployed to Fly.io with Litestream streaming backups to R2. Full teardown-and-restore drill took 4 minutes. A community college IT department could run this on a spare VM.
